items_fr: I. Prend acte avec satisfaction du rapport du Secretaire general;

2. Se felicite de  !'adoption a l'unanimite par le Conseil de securite, le 29 avril 1991, de la resolution 690 (l 991), par laquelle le  Conseil a  approuve le rapport que lui a presente le  Secretaire general le  19  avril  199 t 1·  et a decide de  consti› tuer, sous son  autorite, une Mission des Nations Unies pour !'organisation d'un referendum au  Sahara occidental;

3. Se felicite  egalement de l'entree en vigueur, le 6 sep› tembre  1991,  du  cessez-le-feu  au  Sahara occidental,  con› formement a la proposition du  Secretaire general acceptee par le  Royaume  du  Maroc  et  le  Frente  Popular  para la Liberaci6n de  Saguia el-Hamra y de Rio de Oro;

4. Appuie la demande que le Conseil de securite a faite aux deux parties de continuer a cooperer pleinement avec le Secretaire  general a la mise en ceuvre  de  son plan tel  que decrit dans son rappmt du  18 j uin  1990 10 et developpe dans son rapport du 19 avril 199 l ;
5. Rend hommage au  Secretaire general pour son action en  vue  du reglement de  la  question  du  Sahara  occidental par la mise en ceuvre de son  plan de paix;

6. Appuie pleinement !'action menee  par le  Secretaire general pour  assurer !'organisation et le  controle par I 'Or› ganisation des Nations Unies, en  cooperation avec !'Orga› nisation  de  !'unite africaine,  d'un referendum  d'autodeter› mination  du  peuple  du  Sahara  occidental,  conformement aux objectifs  enonces dans son rapport du  19 av1il  199 l;

items_en: I. Takes note with appreciation of the report of the Secretary-General;

2. Welcomes the unanimous adoption by the Security Council on 29 April 1991 of Resolution 690 (l 991), by which the Council approved the report submitted to it by the Secretary-General on 19 April 199 t 1 and decided to carry out the killing, under his authority, of a United Nations Mission for the organization of a referendum in Western Sahara;

3. Also welcomes the entry into force, on 6 September 1991, of the ceasefire in Western Sahara, in accordance with the proposal of the Secretary General accepted by the Kingdom of Morocco and the Frente Popular para la Liberaci6n de Saguia el-Hamra y de Rio de Oro;

4. Supports the request of the Security Council to the two parties to continue to cooperate fully with the Secretary General in the implementation of its plan as set out in its report of 18 June 1990 10 and elaborated in its report of 19 April 199 l;
5. Commends the Secretary General for his efforts to resolve the question of Western Sahara through the implementation of his peace plan;

6. Supports fully !'action led by the Secretary General to ensure !'organisation and control by the Organization of the United Nations, in cooperation with !'Orga› nisation de !'unite africaine, d'une referendum d'autodeter› mination du peuple du Sahara occidentale, in accordance with the objectives set out in its report of 19 April 199 l;

Requests the Special Committee on the Situation with regard to the Implementation of the Declaration on Independence to Colonial Countries and Peoples to continue to examine the situation in Western Sahara, bearing in mind the ongoing referendum process, and to report thereon to the General Assembly at its forty-second session;
8. Invites the Secretary-General to submit a report on the implementation of the present resolution at its forty-seventh session.

items_fr: I. items_en: I. Approves the chapter in the report of the Special Committee on the Situation with regard to the Declaration on the Granting of Independence to Colonial Countries and Peoples relating to Anguilla, Bermuda, Guam, the Cayman Islands, the Turks and Caicos Islands, the Ame1icaine Virgin Islands, the British Virgin Islands, Montserrat, American Samoa and the To› kelaou1~;

2. Reaffirms the inalienable right of the peoples of those Territories to self-determination and independence, in accordance with the Charter of the United Nations and its resolution 1514 (XV), containing the Declaration on the Granting of Independence to Colonial Countries and Peoples;
3. Reaffirms also that it is in the end to the populations of ccs territories themselves that it belongs

to determine freely their future political status, in accordance with the relevant provisions of the United Nations Charter, the Declaration and the relevant resolutions of the General Assembly, and to this end to request the administering authorities, in cooperation with the Governments of those Territories, to facilitate the realization of political education programmes in these tenitories in order to make their populations aware of the options available to them to exercise their right to selfdetermination, taking into account the different legitimate political statutes clearly defined in Resolution 1541 (XV) of the General Assembly;

4. Reaffirms that it is the responsibility of the administering Powers to create in these territories the conditions necessary for their peoples to exercise freely and without interference their unalienable right to self-detennination and independence;

5. Reaffirms egaleme111 quec of factors such as the nature of the ten-itoire, the geographical situation, the importance of the population and the limitation of natural resources should in no way prevent the populations of these ten-itories from being rapidly affected by their ina›able right to self-determination;

6. Reaffirms that, under the terms of the Charle, it is incumbent upon the administering Powers to promote economic and social development and to preserve the cultural identity of those Territories and recommends that the continuing priority, in consultation with the governments of the territories concerned, be given to the strengthening and diversification of their economies;

7. Requests the administering Powers, in cooperation with the Governments of the Territories concerned, to take or continue to take effective measures to safeguard and guarantee the inalienable right of the people of those Territories to possess them, to set aside or dispose of natural resources, including marine resources, and to establish and maintain their authority over their subsequent exploitation;

8. Requests the same i11sta111e11t the administrative powers› to take all necessary measures to protect the environment of the ten-ten-toire places under Jur ad›ministration and to preserve it from any degradation and asks the competent specialized institutions to continue to monitor the state of the environment in these ten-i› tors;
9. Calls upon the administering Powers to continue to take all necessary measures, in cooperation with the Governments of those tenitories, to address the problems related to drug trafficking:

10. Urges the administering Powers to encourage or continue to encourage the maintenance of close relations between the Territories and other island communities in their respective regions, and to encourage cooperation in the governance of the regional tenitories and agencies, as well as the specialized and organizational institutions of the United Nations;

11. Also urges the administering Powers to cooperate or continue to cooperate with the Special Committee in the discharge of its mandate by providing, in due course and in accordance with the provisions of Article 7 (3) of the Charter, updated information for "~:v 1 11th day, readin Sllls place and facilitate the administration of

text_fr: tant  l 'envoi dans ces teJTitoires  de missions de visite char› gees d 'obtenir des renseignements de premiere main et de s'enque1ir des vreux et des aspirations de leurs habitants;
text_en: both the sending of visiting missions to these territories to obtain first-hand information and to investigate the vortices and aspirations of their inhabitants;

items_fr: 12. Demande i11sta111111ent aux  puissances  administrantes de continuer ou de rccommencer a participer aux seances et activites futures du Comite special et d'assurer la participa› tion de representants des tenitoires non autonomes aux tra› vaux du Comite special;

13. Exhorte les  Etats Membres a participer aux efforts deployes par !'Organisation des Nations Unies pour realiser I 'elimination du colonialisme d'ici a l'an 2000 et les engage a continuer  d'appuyer sans  reserve  l'action entreprise  a cette fin par le Comite special;

14. Invite lcs institutions specialisees et Jes  autres orga› nismes des Nations Unies a prendre ou a continuer de pren› dre toutes les mesures necessaires pour accelerer le progres economique et social de ces teJTitoires;

15. Frie les  institutions  specialisees et les  autres orga› nismes des Nations Unies de  tenir dilment compte, en for› mulant lcurs programmes d'assistance, du document intitule « Problcmcs et  perspectives : schema de strategic», adopte ,i l'unauimite par  la  Reunion  d'experts gouvernementaux des pays en dcveloppcment insulaires et des pays et  orga› nismcs donateurs 1 ·:

16. Frie le  Comite  special de  continuer a examiner la question des petits territoires et de recommander a l' Assem› blee  generale  lcs  mesures  les  plus  aptes a pennettre aux populations de ces ten-itoires d 'exercer leur droit a l'autode› te1mination et a l'independance et de presenter un rapport a cc sujet a I' Assemblee !ors de sa  quarante-septieme session.
items_en: 12. Requests the administering Powers to continue or begin to participate in the future meetings and activities of the Special Committee and to ensure the participation of representatives of the non-self-governing tenitories in the work of the Special Committee;

13. Urges Member States to participate in the efforts of the United Nations to achieve the elimination of colonialism by the year 2000 and calls upon them to continue to give unreserved support to the efforts of the Special Committee to that end;
14. Invites the specialized institutions and other bodies of the United Nations to take or to continue to take all necessary measures to accelerate the economic and social progress of these bodies;

15. Fries to the specialized institutions and other bodies of the United Nations to take into account the document entitled "Problems and perspectives: schema de strategy", adopted unanimously by the Meeting of Governmental Experts of the Island Developing Countries and Countries, and to organise donors 1 ·:

16. Fries the Special Committee to continue to examine the question of the small territories and to recommend to the Assembly the measures most suitable for the people of these territories to exercise their right to self-determination and independence and to submit a report to the Assembly at its forty-seventh session.

items_fr: I. Encourage le Gouvemement de la Nouvelle-Zelande, Puissance  administrante,  a continuer  de  respecter  entiere› ment  les  vceux  de  la  population  tokelaouane  en  gerant  le developpement politique et economique du  ten-itoire de fa9on a preserver son patrimoine social et culture! et ses tradi› tions;

Invite toutes  les  organisations  gouvcrnementales  et non  gouvemementales,  toutes  Jes  institutions  financieres, tousles Etats Membres et tous les organismes des Nations Unies a apporter  ou a continuer d'apporter aux  Tokelaou une  aide  economique d'urgence pour attenuer les effets du cyclone Ofa  et permettre au  territoire  de  satisfaire  ses  be› soins a moyen et a long terme en matiere de relevement et de reconstruction.
items_en: I. Encourages the New Zealand Government, as the administering Power, to continue to respect fully the wishes of the Tokelauan population by managing the political and economic development of the island to preserve its social and cultural heritage and its traditions;
